_purpose: Defines the pilot cohort, staged activation order, per-inbox kill switches, and rollback states. Use this file to control exactly which inboxes are active at any given time and track the rollout status of each phase per talent.
_update_instructions: Update the 'status' field for each talent as you activate their scenarios. Do not activate a phase for a talent until the previous phase is confirmed stable.

pilot_cohort._comment: Katrina's inbox (katrina@taboost.me) is the sole active test inbox for the initial trial. All other inboxes remain inactive until Katrina's pilot is confirmed stable.

rationale.Katrina: Only real email address available for testing right now (katrina@taboost.me). Dual escalation logic makes it a good stress-test for routing rules.

success_criteria: Katrina's inbox fires the scenario for at least 5 real emails
Triage scores visible in master log — no blank rows
Score 1 emails appear in Gmail Trash (not inbox)
Score 2 and 3 emails logged with correct labels
Score 3 emails produce a Gmail Draft in Katrina's inbox (not sent — Draft only)
No error alert emails received during the 48-hour pilot
Marco reviews drafts and confirms triage + draft quality is acceptable

activation_order._comment: After the pilot cohort is confirmed stable, activate the remaining inboxes in these batches. Wait at least 24 hours between batches to catch issues before they spread to all inboxes.

batch_1_post_pilot.description: High-GMV fashion and beauty creators. Activate after pilot is confirmed stable.
batch_1_post_pilot.prerequisite: Pilot inbox (Katrina) confirmed stable for 48 hours

batch_4_special.description: Special handling required (dual manager escalation, hourly rate). Activate last — after all standard inboxes are stable and the confidence_policy.json special talent routing is confirmed working.
batch_4_special.prerequisite: Batch 3 confirmed stable. Katrina and Michaela dual-manager logic tested manually.

inbox_status._comment: Track the current activation status of each inbox per phase. Update these values as you activate each scenario. Statuses: inactive, pilot, active, paused, disabled.

_statuses.inactive: Not yet activated. Scenario exists but is off in Make.
_statuses.pilot: Active in Make but only being monitored — no autonomous replies yet.
_statuses.paused: Temporarily suspended (e.g. talent requested pause, or investigating an issue). Will resume.
_statuses.disabled: Permanently stopped (e.g. talent left the agency).

Trin.notes: Not in pilot — activate in Batch 1 after Katrina pilot is confirmed stable

Sam.notes: Not in pilot — activate in Batch 1 after Katrina pilot is confirmed stable

Allee.notes: Multi-account complexity — review confidence_policy.json before Phase 2

Katrina.notes: ACTIVE TEST INBOX — katrina@taboost.me. Phase 2 in DRAFT MODE (saves drafts, does not send). Dual manager escalation — set Auto-Respond Flag = ESCALATE for all rows until routing confirmed

Colleen.notes: Not in pilot — activate in Batch 1 after Katrina pilot is confirmed stable

KatrinaD.notes: Hourly rate model — activate last, after special rate logic is confirmed

Michaela.notes: Highest rates + dual manager — activate last, after special routing logic is confirmed

kill_switches._comment: Procedures to quickly pause or disable individual inboxes without affecting others. Each inbox has its own independent scenario in Make — pausing one does not affect the others.

steps: 1. Go to Make → Scenarios.
2. Find the Phase 1 scenario for the affected talent (e.g. 'Phase 1 — Email Triage — Trin').
3. Click the toggle to turn the scenario OFF.
4. Update config/rollout_controls.json → inbox_status → [talent] → phase1_triage to 'paused'.
5. Note the reason in the 'notes' field for that talent.
6. The talent's inbox will continue receiving emails in Gmail — they are just not being auto-processed.
7. When ready to resume: turn the scenario back ON. Update status to 'active'.
1. Go to Make → Scenarios.
2. Select all Phase 1 scenarios (use the checkbox on the scenario list page).
3. Click 'Deactivate' to turn all off simultaneously.
4. Also deactivate the Phase 2 and Phase 3 scenarios.
5. Note the reason and timestamp in inbox_status notes for each talent.
6. No emails will be processed until scenarios are reactivated.
7. Resume by reactivating the pilot cohort first, then the other batches in activation order.
pause_all_inboxes.when_to_use: Full pause is appropriate when: a critical bug is discovered, OpenAI or Make is experiencing widespread issues, a bad reply is found and the root cause is unknown.

steps: 1. Go to Make → Scenarios → find 'Phase 2 — Auto-Reply Engine'.
2. Turn the scenario OFF.
3. Phase 1 triage continues to run — emails are still scored and logged.
4. All Score 3 emails are logged as 'queued for reply' but no replies are sent.
5. The supervisor manually reviews queued emails in the master log.
6. Resume Phase 2 after fixing the root cause.
disable_phase2_only.when_to_use: Disable Phase 2 only when reply quality issues are found but triage is still working correctly.

rollback_checklist._comment: If a phase needs to be rolled back completely (not just paused), follow this checklist.
rollback_phase2_to_phase1_only: Turn off Phase 2 (Auto-Reply Engine) scenario in Make.
Review the master log for any emails that are stuck in 'queued for reply' status.
For each stuck email: manually decide whether to reply, ignore, or move to uncertain.
Update the log rows to reflect the manual action taken.
Investigate and fix the root cause.
Run tests/reply_test_cases.json to validate the fix.
Reactivate Phase 2 starting with the pilot inbox (Katrina).
Keep 15-minute send delay active after rollback — do not disable until 5 clean days pass.
rollback_phase1_completely: Turn off ALL Phase 1 scenarios in Make (see pause_all_inboxes above).
Turn off Phase 2 and Phase 3 scenarios.
Access each talent Gmail inbox directly and process emails manually.
Investigate the failure using the Error Log tab in the master log sheet.
Run node scripts/preflight_validator.js and confirm all checks pass.
Run tests/triage_test_cases.json to validate triage quality.
Monitor for 24 hours before reactivating all inboxes.

qa_period.description: During the 5-day QA period (Days 24-28 per the build plan), the supervisor reviews 100% of outbound replies BEFORE marking the system fully autonomous. The 15-minute send delay provides the review window.
daily_qa_checklist: Open the master log — check all rows with Action Taken = 'replied' from the past 24 hours.
For each reply: verify the reply text was sent correctly (spot-check 3-5 Gmail sent folders).
Check for any placeholder text ([BRAND_NAME], [TALENT_NAME]) in sent replies.
Check for any internal routing language ('CC Cara', 'Move to Revisit') in sent replies.
Check that no physical addresses appeared in sent replies.
Log any issues found in the 'Override Queue' sheet (see sheets/override_queue_template.csv).
If zero issues found for 5 consecutive days: sign off on full autonomous operation.
After sign-off: reduce send delay to 0 in config/settings.json → reply.send_delay_minutes.

sign_off.condition: Zero reply quality issues found across all 5 QA days
action_after_sign_off: Update config/settings.json → reply.send_delay_minutes to 0 (or agreed reduced delay)
Update all inbox_status entries to 'active' in this file
Notify the team that the system is fully autonomous
